documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Adult Social Care Accelerator Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and full guide for the Cúram 8.1.2 Adult Social Care accelerator. It states the guide applies to Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2, then outlines the document’s roadmap: accelerator overview and business scenario, installation/build prerequisites, XML server startup, a caseworker walkthrough, fit-gap and requirements analysis, and step-by-step application building. The accelerator is a worked social program example for incapacity benefits, using Case Management, Intake, Intelligent Evidence Gathering, Express Rules, and Workflow. The scenario covers a client moving from Sickness Benefit to Invalidity Benefit after six months, with tasks such as application creation, evidence management, authorization, payment generation, and certificate extension. Later sections cover configuring user views, evidence, products, intake, person match, and workflows, including custom event/task handling. The end of the section contains legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Appeals Developer Guide
documents.summary: This section is the front matter and core developer guide for Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2 Appeals. It explains how to extend Merative SPM Appeals beyond the default Product Delivery, Issue, and Integrated Case types. Key topics include enabling appeal of case objects via the Appealable interface, adding ct_AppealObjectType code table entries, binding implementations with Guice, and implementing a 3-step client wizard that passes selected objects as ObjectID,ObjectTypeCode. It also covers enabling appeals for another case type with the AppealableCaseType interface, including appeal eligibility, continue-benefits logic, wizard hooks, Guice binding, and updating Appeals Client navigation/menu configuration. Front matter includes notices, edition, contents, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Authoring Scripts using Intelligent Evidence Gathering Guide
documents.summary: Comprehensive guide to authoring Cúram 8.1.2 Intelligent Evidence Gathering (IEG) scripts. It covers the script lifecycle from basics and prerequisites (XML, databases, stylesheets) through script structure (scripts, sections, pages, clusters, questions, subscripts), element reference, expression operators and custom functions, flow control and conditional navigation, layout/configuration options, administration/import/testing, and compliance rules. Also includes legal notices and permissions. Useful if you need to create, customize, administer, or upgrade IEG scripts, understand supported XML elements and expressions, or follow constraints on internal IDs, public API use, source control, and non-customizable model files.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Batch Process Reference Guide
documents.summary: Reference guide for Cúram 8.1.2 batch processing. It lists the default batch jobs available in Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2 and explains the common `processingDate` parameter, batch streaming, and Batch Process Instance ID usage for concurrent runs. The section covers core processes such as MeetingManagement, eligibility/reassessment, communication and closure-related jobs, provider management, decision assist, financial processing, funded program management, purges, import/export, and milestone delivery scans. Individual entries typically include purpose, class/method, parameters, defaults, dependencies, and execution notes, including ordering constraints like running TransferInstructionLineItems immediately after GenerateInstructionLineItems. Also includes front-matter legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks, with the main substantive content spanning pages 9–66.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Business Intelligence and Analytics Guide
documents.summary: Intro/front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Business Intelligence and Analytics Guide. It identifies the guide’s scope and audience, then explains BI&A at a business level: why SEM agencies use it to measure performance, detect process gaps, and support decision-making. The section outlines the main BI elements—data warehousing, embedded analytics, ad hoc reporting with BIRT, and interactive dashboards/reports—and describes the BI infrastructure: reporting schema, ETL/extraction flow, central data warehouse, datamarts, and optional viewers such as Cognos. It also covers how to define reporting requirements, model reporting data, generate initial reports, and use change data capture for warehouse refreshes. The remaining pages are notices and legal/front-matter content: permissions, warranty/disclaimer language, export and patent notes, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support Cash Assistance Program Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and opening section of the Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support Cash Assistance Program Guide. It identifies the document as a business analyst reference for Cash Assistance eligibility, explaining that eligibility is determined from captured evidence and business rules for needy families with dependent children. The section outlines the main rule areas covered in the guide: non-financial requirements (citizenship/alienage, SSN, residency), assistance unit and household composition rules, dependent child and head-of-household determinations, program eligibility limits such as striker status and the 60-month federal time limit, alien sponsor deeming, resource and income tests, benefit calculation, and sanctions. Sanctions discussed include child support, immunization, minor parent living/school requirements, school attendance, voluntary quit, work registration, intentional program violation, fleeing felon, parole violation, fraud, and drug-related felony. The remaining pages are legal notices, privacy policy, trademarks, and copyright/license information.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Common Intake Configuration Guide
documents.summary: This section is the Common Intake configuration guide for Cúram 8.1.2. It explains how intake behavior is defined in XML and stored in the IntakeProcessName and IntakeProcessConfiguration tables, then assigned to users/roles via application codes, including a required DefaultApp mapping. It covers configuring supported programs and their display descriptions, initial contact and intake summary behavior, cases view and quick search panels, workflow customization, generic PDF summary forms, triage and screening definitions, urgent alerts and program recommendations, and client application setup. It also details Universal Access integration, transfer/add-to-existing-case rules, case status changes after transfer or undo, TODO list context, and application quick search preview panels. The last part lists common intake application properties for registrars, search/transfer enablement, map defaults, milestone updates, and work-queue routing.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Citizen Context Viewer Configuration Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and chapter 1 of the Cúram 8.1.2 Citizen Context Viewer Configuration Guide. It introduces CCV configuration for displaying client information in care and protection, communities, dealings, family, and multidisciplinary teams, and explains that most settings are dynamic application properties integrated with Cúram security and case settings. The guide describes the administrator’s role, dependencies between settings, and how to configure supported case types, case/service plan/social enterprise folder types, statuses, and participant roles, plus community and dealings display options. It includes a summary table of CCV application properties. The remainder is legal front matter: a note to read Notices, edition applicability for Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2, contents, and the Merative notices, privacy policy, and trademarks pages.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Cúram Express Rules Reference Manual
documents.summary: This node is the full Cúram 8.1.2 Cúram Express Rules Reference Manual. It introduces CER as a rule-based language and runtime for business calculations, with an editor and testing environment, and explains core concepts like rule sets, rule classes, objects, attributes, sessions, XML storage, validation, and runtime recalculation. Major topics include developing and testing rule sets, localizing and validating rules, generating documentation and coverage reports, handling data and time-based changes, Dependency Manager recalculation and batch/deferred processing, editor and palette references, best practices, XML dictionary elements, list operations, datastore integration, and compliancy rules. It also covers legal notices, privacy, and trademarks. Useful if you need the overall CER architecture, authoring workflow, runtime behavior, supported APIs/tables, or compliance constraints.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Business Intelligence and Analytics for Cúram Child Services Guide
documents.summary: Introduces Cúram Child Services Business Intelligence and Analytics. The section explains the three-part BI architecture: a domain-specific reporting schema, ETL infrastructure, and business process support, all sourced from the Cúram operational database. It outlines the three tiers used across schema and ETL: staging, central data warehouse, and data mart. The content highlights child welfare reporting use cases such as contact compliance, placement stability, and maltreatment recurrence, with sample data elements including contact type/purpose/frequency and placement/removal dates. It also describes report configuration options for administrators, including contact compliance settings in CFSS, maltreatment recurrence window and target parameters, and the days-in-placement threshold. The rest of the pages are legal/front-matter content: notices, usage permissions, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Child Services Business Guide
documents.summary: This section is the full front matter and main introductory content for the Cúram 8.1.2 Child Services Business Guide. It introduces CCS for child welfare agencies and explains its purpose, audience, and background. Key topics include supporting caseworkers, intake/referral, investigation, removal and return, outcome management, ongoing case management, adoption, performance indicators, and scenario-based walkthroughs. It also covers caseworker tools such as home pages, prior history, common intake, correspondence, contacts, multidisciplinary/legal actions, eligibility/background information, and inter-jurisdictional handling. The included scenarios illustrate child protection, family services, information and referral, inter-jurisdictional placement, and adoption workflows. The end of the section contains legal notices, privacy policy, trademarks, copyright, and sample-code disclaimers.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Citizen Context Viewer Developer Guide
documents.summary: Introductory guide for customizing the Cúram 8.1–8.1.2 Citizen Context Viewer (CCV). It explains the guide’s purpose, intended audience, prerequisites, and structure, then shows how to add UIM links that launch CCV, customize CCV through injection points/events, write loaders and case handlers, define node types and right-click menu options with XML examples, and localize non-database CCV text via message files. It also includes compliance guidance: use only the documented public API, avoid dependencies on undocumented classes/packages, and expect non-API elements to change without notice. The end of the section contains legal notices, privacy policy, and trademark/copyright information.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Citizen Context Viewer Overview Guide
documents.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Citizen Context Viewer Overview Guide, covering edition, contents, legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks. The substantive guide explains the Citizen Context Viewer (CCV) as a pop-up, tree-based, client-centered view that consolidates client, family, community, dealings, case, and multidisciplinary team information across agencies. It describes CCV architecture and components, including care and protection, communities, family, dealings, and MDT views; customization of displayed data and components; viewing family-member information; and security controls based on case, service plan, client sensitivity, and team-member sensitivity. Useful for understanding CCV purpose, navigation, configuration, and access rules.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Common Intake Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and full contents for the Cúram 8.1.2 Common Intake Guide. It introduces Common Intake (CCI) as a configurable “no wrong door” intake framework for matching client needs to programs and community services, aimed at business analysts in social enterprise organizations. The guide covers the end-to-end intake flow: client search and registration, triage, screening for potential eligibility, application creation and management, life events, referrals, and case worker tools such as advisor, notes, PCR, and alerts. It also explains how agencies configure intake processes, including triage, screening, applications, life events, PCR rules/ranges, and referrals. The section includes the table of contents plus legal notices, privacy policy, and trademark information, with copyright and usage terms from Merative US L.P. (2012, 2024).

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Configuring Evidence Guide
documents.summary: This guide explains how to configure evidence in Cúram 8.1.2. It covers evidence type setup for caseworkers, including showing deleted evidence in lists, creating case-type-specific Evidence Dashboard groups with required evidence flags, and enabling end dating during evidence creation for dynamic and non-dynamic evidence. A major focus is dynamic evidence administration: evidence types vs. versions, lifecycle and activation, metadata evolution, security administration, editor and model/UI setup, multiple participant updates, rule sets, localization, extractors/loaders, generated artefacts, application properties, and compliance constraints. It also includes sample widget/domain setup and generated resource details. The front matter contains edition info, contents, and legal notices/trademark/privacy material.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Content Management Interoperability Services Integration Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and opening chapter of the Cúram 8.1.2 CMIS Integration Guide. It introduces how Cúram integrates with a CMS/CMIS repository for document attachments, Microsoft Word communications, and pro forma communications. Key setup topics include registering a CMS target system, configuring the CMIS service and application properties, encrypted credentials, and the rule that exactly one CMIS target system service must exist. It also covers gradual migration from database storage, required metadata and default metadata, customization of metadata, directory structures and file-naming strategies, and single sign-on support via custom hooks and `curam.cms.sso.enable`. The section ends with CMIS validation/error messages for troubleshooting storage, retrieval, configuration, and connection problems, followed by notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Provider Management Configuration Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and the full “Configuring Provider Management” chapter for Cúram 8.1–8.1.2. It explains CPM runtime configuration for provider administration (external access security, provider email server, contract/license renewal notifications, training/certification setup), service offering administration (registration, financials, grouping/search, approval criteria, keywords/evidence, attendance and absence tracking, payment rules), service evaluation criteria, CPM batch processes, and a detailed summary of application properties. The properties cover attendance, financials, facility characteristics, contracts, licenses, sequential reference numbers, wait list review timing, reservation grace periods, infrastructure/email, case parameters, and dynamic SQL. Pages 29–30 are legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Creating Datastore Schemas Guide
documents.summary: Guide to creating Cúram 8.1–8.1.2 datastore schemas for intake/screening data. Explains what a datastore is, how XML data is shredded into entities, and that datastores must be accessed only through `curam.datastore.impl` (other `curam.datastore.*` packages are not API). Covers schema rules: single XML namespace or none, global-level entity/domain definitions, included schema fragments, allowed datatypes based on `BaseDomains`, supported XML and annotation constraints, entity/attribute structure, and identity constraints using `SVR_KEY` with `key`/`keyref`. Also includes compliance guidance on using only documented public API, plus front matter, notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Merative ™ Creating external applications with the Cúram Web Design System
documents.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 guide “Creating external applications with the Cúram Web Design System,” covering pages 1–12. It includes the title/edition pages, a note directing readers to the legal notices, and the table of contents. The main substantive content is Chapter 1, which introduces the Cúram Web Design System as a React-based framework for building responsive, accessible external web apps with REST API integration and theming. It explains three persona paths: citizen benefit applications, authorized representative/supporter applications, and non-benefits apps built from scratch. It also notes that citizen/representative solutions depend on Cúram Platform plus Universal Access and the Web Design System, while other external apps require only the Platform and the Web Design System. The remaining pages contain legal notices: usage permissions, export and redistribution restrictions, “AS-IS” warranty disclaimers, patent and third-party product notices, privacy policy, trademarks, and sample code license terms.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Business Object Module Development Guide
documents.summary: Introductory guide for Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2 on developing Business Object Modules (BOMs) for Configuration Transport Manager (CTM). It defines Business Object Type vs. Business Object, explains CTM release/apply transport flow, and outlines BOM responsibilities and infrastructure (including Range Aware Key Server and runtime data). The chapter covers the 11 BOM interfaces, noting which have out-of-the-box implementations, and gives implementation guidance through a worked example using Folder and User business object types. It also discusses testing transport, assumptions about required facades/adapters/DAOs/code-table classes, and how to customize revert Change Set construction with RevertChangeSetConstructionHandlerBOM. Front matter includes note, edition, contents, and legal notices/privacy/trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Curam Accessibility User Guide
documents.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Accessibility User Guide. It includes a note to read the Notices section first, edition/copyright information, and the table of contents. The main content starts with an accessibility overview stating support for WCAG 2.1 AA, Section 508, and EN 301 549, plus supported technologies and keyboard/screen reader guidance. It documents desktop accessibility for multiple-select and single-select lists, the section shortcut panel, tabs, Rich Text Editor, and the classic IEG date picker. It also provides accessible alternatives or workarounds for the Calendar, Express Rule Editor, Data Store Editor, Intelligent Evidence Gathering editor/scripts, browser zoom at 200%, planning workspace, and non-accessible pods. The section ends with legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Getting Started Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and overview for the Cúram 8.1.2 Getting Started Guide. It introduces how to begin working with Cúram across the main lifecycle areas: analysis/configuration/customization, business analysis, configuration, development, security, deployment, upgrade, and administration. Key points include the distinction between configuration (no coding) and customization (coding), use of CADT-generated analysis documentation and ERDs for business/technical analysis, role-based configuration administration, development prerequisites such as the Cúram Development Environment and compliant development guidelines, deployment to an application server, upgrade aids like the Cúram Upgrade Pack and UI Upgrade Helper, and initial production administration before go-live. The rest is legal material: notices, privacy policy, and trademarks, including Merative copyright and usage restrictions.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Development Environment Installation Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and the full “Installing a development environment” guide for Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2. It covers supported development setups (light, full, build, and supplemental front-end), Windows development support and Linux build support, and prerequisites such as Ant, Java SE/EE, IBM Installation Manager, application servers (WebSphere/WebLogic), and databases (H2, Db2, Oracle). It explains installing Cúram software, applying fixes, postinstallation configuration, environment variables, database setup, build/test steps, and security-related configuration. It also includes Eclipse/Tomcat setup, Rational Software Architect Designer plug-ins and workspace setup, starting the server/Tomcat/RMILoginClient, logging in, deployment URLs and roles, and upgrade guidance via the Cúram Upgrade Helper Pack and UI Upgrade Helper. The section ends with legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Product Overview Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and opening section of the Cúram 8.1.2 Product Overview Guide. It identifies the guide scope (applies to Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2), then introduces Cúram as a configurable social enterprise platform for human services, health, labor, social security, and military/veterans programs supporting Beveridge and Bismarck models. The section outlines core architecture and platform capabilities: social programs data model, intelligent evidence gathering, decision assist, financial management, funded program management, supervisor workspace, integrated case management, intake, eligibility and entitlement, evidence management, participant management, and major modules such as Appeals, BI&A, Evidence Broker, Provider Management, Universal Access, Verification Engine, and Income Support. It also covers technical architecture (ADE, runtime/development architecture, business infrastructure), tablet support, deprecated and removed functionality, and PDF access/prebuilt PDFs. The final pages contain legal notices, privacy policy, and trademark/copyright information.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 What's New Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and legal pages for the Cúram 8.1.2 What’s New Guide, covering editions for Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2. It includes the table of contents, a release overview, and the main “What’s new” section summarizing updates across these versions. Key topics include CSRF-protected web navigation docs, enhanced text translation, external application framework improvements, IEG script guidance, new financial hook points, AppResource documentation, product rebranding to Cúram by Merative, accessibility improvements, dropdown and sortable-column UX enhancements, Person Evidence Management, context panel refresh guidance, Azure SSO/OIDC setup, AKS support for development/test, and deprecated feature removals. It also contains a brief release-notes pointer advising review before installation/configuration, plus full notices, privacy policy, and trademarks/legal terms.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Address Guide
documents.summary: Overview of the Cúram 8.1.2 Address Guide and its legal front matter. The guide explains how participant addresses are used in registration, duplicate checking, participant search, contacts, communications, and payments/invoices. It covers primary vs secondary addresses, address types (postal, web, email), which participant types must have postal addresses, and how addresses are created, viewed, modified, canceled, and kept with history in Participant Manager. It also includes geocoding/mapping support (Google Maps APIs and related configuration properties) and configurable address formats via curam-config.xml. The front matter includes the note to read Notices, edition info for Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2, table of contents, privacy policy, trademarks, and detailed Merative usage/legal notices.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Advisor Configuration Guide
documents.summary: This section is the front matter plus Chapter 1 of the Cúram 8.1.2 Advisor Configuration Guide. It explains how to configure Cúram Advisor for context-sensitive advice: define advice contexts, add page IDs and page parameters, and assign CER rule sets. It describes the required rule class structure for advisor rule sets, including AbstractAdviceContext and AbstractAdviceItem, and the key attributes such as advice, adviceText, showAdvice, category, priority, and status. It also covers displaying advice text and links, managing evidence rule object conversion/propagation for active/in-edit succession sets, and includes a worked SpouseRuleSet example that shows advice on the Person home page when a person is married and has no spouse relationship. The section also contains the document contents and legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Appeals Guide
documents.summary: This node is the full Cúram Appeals Guide for version 8.1.2. It introduces the appeals and legal actions module, covering appeal concepts and process stages (hearing, hearing review, judicial review), appellants/respondents, multiple appeals, legal petitions, hearings, orders, user/participant roles, lifecycle flows, deadlines, correspondence, data maintenance, and administration. It also includes configurable appeal setup topics such as security, process levels/types, time constraints, clauses, legal action types/categories, and legal statuses. The section is largely navigational and reference-oriented, with a detailed table of contents and end matter, plus legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Tuning Batch Streaming Performance Guide
documents.summary: Guide to tuning Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2 batch performance via batch streaming and transaction-level caching. Explains the two-executable streaming model (Chunker and parallel Stream processes), chunk creation and commit-point processing, optional serial processing of leftover unprocessed records, and dynamic stream coordination. Lists streamed batch programs such as Generate Instruction Line Items, Generate Instruments, CREOLE Bulk Case Chunk Reassessment By Product, Apply Product Reassessment Strategy, Perform Batch Recalculations From Precedent Change Set, and related translation/redetermination processing. Covers how to run streamed batch executables and tune behavior with environment variables for chunk size, wait intervals, whether to run/sleep for streams, and whether to process unprocessed chunks. Includes error handling for skipped chunks and skipped records, distinguishing business errors (record removed and task created) from technical errors (record retained for rerun). Also contains front matter, contents, notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Batch Processes Developer Guide
documents.summary: This section is the front matter and opening chapter of the Cúram 8.1.2 Batch Processes Developer Guide. It introduces Cúram SDEJ batch processing and how to define batch operations in UML, including key restrictions: only one batch operation per process class, one flat struct parameter, and a void return type. It documents supported server data types and their string formats, batch process output responsibilities, and the Batch Launcher for running queued or single requests, including startup properties, security, error codes, output directory, debugging, and monitoring. It also covers Batch Administration setup: process definitions, groups, requests, and error code mapping. A major topic is limited DB-to-JMS support for deferred processing/workflow from batch programs, with required properties, deferred-process registration, security requirements, and limitations. The section ends with key-point summary plus legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Batch Streaming Developers Guide
documents.summary: Introductory section for the Cúram 8.1.2 Batch Streaming Developers Guide. It explains the streamed batch model: a chunker identifies independent processing units, groups them into chunks, and stream processes handle each chunk, with restartable processing and reporting afterward. The guide motivates streaming for large volumes and parallelization, and highlights built-in benefits such as commit-per-chunk, automatic skipping/retry handling for errored records, generic logging/reporting, and restartability. It also covers how to design streamed batches by identifying safe processing units and dependencies, including indirect units, extra processing, and batch summary information. Implementation topics include chunker/stream entry points, class/UML structure, processing single records and skipped cases, encoding/decoding chunk results, sending batch reports, and global stream configuration EnvVars. Advanced topics address running multiple chunkers or instances and using composite keys. Front matter and legal notices are included.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Business Intelligence Development Compliancy Guide
documents.summary: Overview of Cúram 8.1.2 BI development compliancy guidance for 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2. It explains how to set up BI projects under source control, tag artifacts by application version, and use file comparisons during upgrades. It documents the client/server and Reporting directory structures and emphasizes that default BI artifacts should not be modified in place; custom work should go into custom files/components using project-specific prefixes and numeric IDs. The guide covers recommended extension points for entities/DDL, initial/demo data, ETL metadata, scripts, Java, and build extensions, plus API rules distinguishing internal vs external APIs. It also lists discouraged practices such as direct entity/ETL/BIRT/schema edits, adding data to default entities, overriding ETL processes, and depending on sample/demo artifacts. Front matter includes note, notices, privacy policy, and trademarks/legal terms.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Business Intelligence Reporting Developer Guide
documents.summary: Introductory BI reporting developer guide for Cúram 8.1–8.1.2. It explains the Business Intelligence and Analytics reporting framework: data warehousing concepts, BIRT reporting, architecture/runtime flow, and ETL stages from source to staging, central warehouse, and datamarts. It covers environment setup for DB2/Oracle, installation and configuration, build scripts, property files, database privileges, changed-data capture using required `lastwritten` timestamp columns, globalization, security, troubleshooting, and log4j2 logging setup. It also includes BIBootstrap/BIApplication configuration, patching notes, and sample scripts/configuration examples. The later pages are legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks. Useful if you need end-to-end BI reporting setup, ETL behavior, or troubleshooting guidance.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Business Intelligence BIRT Developer Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and core guide for Cúram 8.1–8.1.2 on Business Intelligence with BIRT. It introduces BIRT as the reporting engine for chart/tabular decision-support content, explains the BIRT stack and system landscape, and covers build/deployment workflow, Eclipse setup, viewing and integrating report content, authentication/entitlements, creating new reports, JDBC/data sources, localization, design standards, compliance, datamart datasource setup for WebLogic/WebSphere, WebSphere logging, troubleshooting, deployment considerations, runtime architecture, and report-viewing dependencies across Application Server, Tomcat BIRT Viewer, and Eclipse. It also includes WebSphere CuramBIRTViewer deployment and session/cookie configuration, HTTP server plugin/update notes, plus performance, scalability, and security guidance. The section ends with legal notices, privacy policy, trademarks, and copyright/license terms.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Cúram Cache Guide
documents.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Cúram Cache Guide. It includes a note pointing readers to Notices on page 19, the edition scope (Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2), and the full table of contents. The main document introduces Cúram Cache as a generic caching service for online and batch Java environments, covering three cache types: global, thread-local, and transaction-local. It previews declarative APP_CACHE configuration, eviction settings, statistics/JMX, shutdown requirements, global cache provider/default group behavior, transactional invalidation guidance, and code samples for loaders, clients, and invalidation. The back matter contains legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Calendaring Configuration Guide
documents.summary: Introductory guide to Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2 calendaring configuration. It explains how administrators/developers configure meetings, meeting minutes, and calendar activities using application properties: emailing meeting requests, sender/reply mailbox settings, attendee and organizer conflict warnings, meeting minutes email delivery, meeting response batch behavior and invalid-message deletion, recurring activity occurrence limits, and the number of days calendar events display. It includes a property summary table and a developer appendix of calendaring customization events/hooks for meeting and meeting-minutes persistence and workflow processing (create/cancel/invite, issue/record/modify/delete minutes, decisions, notes, attendance). Front matter also includes notice, copyright, and usage terms, plus privacy and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Calendaring Guide
documents.summary: Overview of the Cúram 8.1.2 Calendaring Guide. This section introduces the guide’s scope and core calendaring model: user, case, organization, location, and financial/payment calendars. It explains how calendars store events, meetings, activities, holidays, and recurring items, and how they support scheduling across users, cases, and administration. Key functional topics include user calendars with PIM/email invite integration and homepage summaries; case calendars for integrated and product delivery cases; meetings (scheduling, invites, acceptance, minutes, external email); activities (single and recurring, attendees, concerning case/participant, conflict handling); and case reviews, referrals, and product delivery appeals. The latter pages also contain legal notices, copyright, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Case Audits Developer's Guide
documents.summary: This guide explains how to customize Cúram Case Audits for developers and architects. It covers registering a new sampling algorithm by defining a CT_SamplingStrategy entry, implementing the SamplingStrategy interface, and binding it with Guice. It describes dynamic selection queries, including custom UIM pages, use of the Case Audit Query Management API, and how audit coordinators can generate or manually select case lists. It also explains configuring selection queries, including the differences between dynamic and fixed queries, validation, publishing, and the security caution that fixed queries have no out-of-the-box front end. Finally, it introduces the Case Audits web service for importing external case IDs into audit data. Front matter includes notices, edition applicability to Cúram 8.1–8.1.2, and the table of contents.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Case Audits Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and Chapter 1 of the Cúram 8.1.2 Case Audits Guide. Covers what case audits are and why they’re used, distinguishing quantitative audits (compliance/statute/policy) from qualitative audits (case outcomes and performance). Explains audit roles: audit coordinator, auditor, and audit team, plus the audit lifecycle from plan creation to findings and recommendations. Describes audit anatomy, including focus areas, selection queries/criteria, and audit algorithms, and how audits are configured by case type, permissions, and security. Also covers coordinator workflows for creating, maintaining, and tracking audit plans, and auditor workflows for searching, completing audits, recording findings, and managing feedback from case owners/supervisors. Includes notices, privacy policy, trademarks, and legal terms.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Propagating Non Cúram Data For Cúram Express RulesPropagating Non Cúram Data For Cúram Express Rules Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and the main guide for Cúram 8.1.2. It explains how to create initial CER Rule Objects for rate table data loaded outside Cúram APIs, especially bulk-loaded production data. The guide is aimed at system administrators and covers when to run the batch process, how it differs from API-based automatic propagation, and that it must run before system startup or other processing. It notes the process applies only to rate tables and that custom CER storage needs custom handling. It identifies the RuleObjectPropagatorControl table flag used to suppress automatic startup propagation, and the batch program name curam.core.sl.infrastructure.rate.intf.RateCreateInitialRuleObjects.propa with optional rateTableType parameter for all or specific rate tables. The remaining pages are notices, copyright, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support for Medical Assistance Children's Health Insurance Program Guide
documents.summary: Business-level guide to Cúram Income Support for Medical Assistance CHIP. Explains the program purpose and audience, then outlines CHIP as a state/federal children’s coverage program within the Medical Assistance cascading eligibility hierarchy. Key eligibility facts include: children under 19, uninsured, no other comprehensive coverage, open enrollment limits with exceptions (e.g. newborns), 90-day uninsured rule, and financial eligibility based on income only up to 200% FPL with no resource test. Covers household/financial unit and benefit-group rules, premium/copay assessment, managed care vs fee-for-service context, and creation of CHIP product delivery with 12-month certification. Also describes reassessment and change-of-circumstance processing, including income changes, adding/removing members, newborn handling, and end-date/disenrollment rules. The node also contains front matter notes, contents, notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Communications Overview Guide
documents.summary: Overview of Cúram 8.1.2 communications. Explains how communications are incoming or outgoing, tied to a case or participant, and always linked to a correspondent whose contact details may be auto-retrieved or created as a representative role. Describes how case communications appear on case and correspondent lists, while participant communications stay on the participant’s list, plus how client interactions aggregate communications, payments, and calls. Covers communication exceptions that block disallowed contact methods. Introduces four communication types: recorded, email, pro forma, and Microsoft Word. Recorded communications capture outside-system interactions and can integrate attachments with a CMS. Email communications require valid addresses and can be drafted before sending. Pro forma communications use XSL templates, support batch generation, template versioning for reprints, and CMS storage. Word communications are manually editable from Word templates, but become fixed after sending. Also notes template filtering by participant/case and product/person type, and restricted characters for Word template data.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Decision Assist Guide
documents.summary: This 42-page guide introduces Cúram Decision Assist in Cúram 8.1.2. It explains what Decision Assist is, why agencies use it, and how it supports transparent, configurable, rule-based assessments with determination configurations, packages, IEG questionnaires, outcomes, and decision matrices. It covers assessment lifecycle actions such as answering, making, canceling, completing, cloning, comparing decisions over time, plus versioning and localization support. The guide also walks through sample setups for Asthma and Juvenile Detention screening assessments, including case association, questionnaire and matrix creation, release, and linking to assessments. The final pages contain legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Deductions Guide
documents.summary: Overview of the Cúram 8.1.2 Deductions Guide. It explains how deductions reduce benefit payments to recover debts or pay third parties, and distinguishes applied, un-applied, and third-party deductions. It covers administration setup for deduction processing, including product limits (maximum deduction rate, minimum deduction amount, minimum payment amount), deduction type configuration, categories, fixed/variable amount limits, priorities, effective dates, and participant restrictions. It also describes managing deductions on a case: create, activate, deactivate, override limits, cancel, view statuses/history, and workflow events. Further topics include payment simulation and generation rules, how deductions are processed and skipped, and handling third-party payments when recipient accounts end, including cancel/regenerate behavior. The last pages are legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Deploying on Oracle WebLogic Server Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and full guide for deploying Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2 on Oracle WebLogic Server. It covers prerequisite installation assumptions, building application and web services EARs with SDEJ/Ant, environment setup such as WLS_HOME, EAR contents, facade generation, and the H2 limitation. It then explains WebLogic application server configuration from Ant targets, including domain setup, data source, JMS, security, time zone, and server start/stop/logging. The deployment section covers installing/uninstalling EARs, required properties and parameters, changing the SYSTEM user, precompiling JSPs, and testing via SSL login. A manual configuration section details creating a WebLogic domain, JVM settings, JDBC driver replacement, database setup, security/authenticator and TLS 1.2 configuration, JMS resources (queues, topics, connection factories), and manual EAR deployment. The remaining pages are legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Deploying on IBM® WebSphere® Application Server Guide
documents.summary: WebSphere deployment guide for Cúram 8.1.2 (applies to 8.1, 8.1.1, 8.1.2). Covers building deployable EARs with SDEJ Ant targets, including the main application EAR and web services EAR, required WAS_HOME setup, EAR contents, and optional facade generation. Explains WebSphere Application Server configuration: web server plug-ins, security/LDAP and user registry, authentication logging, custom security properties, cryptography, startup/shutdown, application deployment, JSP precompilation, and deployment testing. Includes a long manual configuration walkthrough for console/wsadmin use, Db2 and Oracle data sources, admin and transport security, JAAS, JMS and Service Integration Bus, historical log files, and post-configuration tasks. Also covers Network Deployment basics: creating profiles, federating nodes, node synchronization, BOOTSTRAP_ADDRESS considerations, and deploying on federated nodes. The final pages are legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Development Compliancy Guide
documents.summary: Introductory compliancy guide for Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2. It explains how to start a new project with source control and version tagging, where custom artifacts belong (webclient and EJBServer custom folders), and how to keep customizations upgrade-safe. Key server rules include using project-specific prefixes, avoiding name collisions, using reserved numeric ranges for initial/demo data, not editing delivered files in place, not depending on sample/demo artifacts, and syncing runtime changes back into development. It also covers supported changes for DMX data, code tables, messages, events, preferences, properties, Java APIs, source code, and server extension mechanisms. Client development guidance emphasizes customizing without altering original artifacts. A component section gives access/customization rules for Internal, External, Access Restricted, Modeled, and Non-Modeled classes, plus notes for CDEJ, SDEJ, Persistence Infrastructure, CER, Dependency Manager, and several Cúram components. The section ends with deprecation guidance and full legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Financials Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and main overview for the Cúram 8.1.2 Financials Guide. It introduces Cúram financials as the system for processing payments, liabilities, and payments received for persons/employers, typically requiring a nominee. The section outlines the full guide scope: financial components, instruction line items, instructions, instruments/payslips, generating and maintaining payments and liabilities, deductions and adjustments, payment receipt allocation and suspense handling, batch processes, financial code tables, and customization/hook points. It also references ERP integration and related guides for adapter, integrated ERP operations, case management, and deductions. Includes legal notices, edition applicability (Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, 8.1.2), copyright, privacy policy, and trademarks. Useful as a navigation entry point rather than a substantive procedure section.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Cúram Incremental Modernization and Transformation (IMT) Web Services Cookbook
documents.summary: Front matter and reference guide for Cúram 8.1–8.1.2 IMT Web Services. It targets developers and explains the purpose, audience, and prerequisites, then documents the available XML web services: Register Person, Claim Intake, Evidence Maintenance, Verification, Determination, and Triage. The service chapters include incoming/response parameter mappings, required and optional fields, internal struct mappings, and example XML messages/errors. Useful if you need field-level API details for creating people and cases, managing/activating evidence, verification checks, eligibility determination, or triage decisions. The section also contains Merative legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Integrated Case Management Configuration Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and the main Chapter 1 of the Cúram 8.1.2 Integrated Case Management Configuration Guide. It explains how agencies configure integrated, product, screening, and assessment case types; common settings like names, home pages, dates, grouping, translator checks, display options, and ownership/workflow assignment; plus configuration of products, services/referrals, evidence types, CER/rules, ongoing case management behaviors, and special cautions. It also covers rate tables in depth, including simple vs. complex tables, entity structure, XML format, business processes, and worked examples for ranged tables and tables with subcolumns. The section ends with legal notices, privacy policy, and trademark information.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Integrated Case Management Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and Chapter 1 of the Cúram 8.1.2 Integrated Case Management Guide. It introduces the needs-to-delivery model: assessing needs through triage, screening, and intake; creating integrated cases; delivering benefits via product delivery cases; delivering services and referrals; using the caseworker workspace; and managing ongoing cases (owner/supervisor changes, audits, notes, evidence, legal proceedings, reassessment, suspension/closure/reactivation). It also covers case security at a high level, including function, field, location, program, and sensitivity security. The section includes the table of contents and navigation content, plus legal front matter such as notices, privacy policy, trademarks, copyright, export/warranty disclaimers, and publication-use terms. Useful as an overview and entry point to the guide, not a task-level procedure.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Integrated ERP Operations Guide
documents.summary: Overview of the Cúram 8.1.2 Integrated ERP Operations Guide. This section explains how to configure a Cúram-ERP financial environment, focusing on shared configuration data, administration properties for the financial and master data adapters, codetable alignment, and disabled batch processes handled by the ERP system. It covers synchronization of address formats, banks/branches, currencies, delivery methods, and selected codetables between Cúram and ERP. It also documents runtime and static adapter properties, including tracing, polling, adapter enablement, registrar settings, and external tax authority handling. The later pages are legal and reference material: usage notices, copyright/trademark information, privacy policy, and license terms.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Investigations Guide
documents.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Investigations Guide, including the title, edition notice, contents, and legal notices. The useful content begins with an overview of Cúram Investigations: what an investigation is, how it is created as an integrated-case or standalone investigation, how ownership is assigned/transferred, and the main lifecycle steps from recording allegations and findings through resolution, approval, closure/reopening, and overriding findings on reopened cases. It also introduces investigation tools such as action plans, milestones, contact log, status history, translator need, legal actions/status, and extra tools. The section further covers configuration topics for investigation types, ownership strategies, milestones and milestone associations, resolutions, assessments, and approval checks. Pages 29–30 contain permissions, warranty/export disclaimers, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Issue Management Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and introduction for the Cúram 8.1.2 Issue Management Guide. It identifies the guide’s purpose and audience, explains the case-based issue management model, and outlines the full lifecycle: configuring issue types, ownership, resolutions, and approval checks; creating, assigning, resolving, approving, and closing issues; tracking statuses, history, workflow events, and appeals. It also covers supporting features such as My Issues, attachments, communications, notes, tasks, and user roles. The section includes the table of contents, edition applicability for Cúram 8.1–8.1.2, and legal material: copyright, usage permissions, warranties/disclaimers, export and patent notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Cúram JMX Configuration Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and the main chapter for Cúram 8.1.2 JMX configuration. It explains how JMX provides runtime operational statistics and how administrators can enable and tune monitoring without code changes. Key topics include core properties for enabling JMX, configuring EJB/Web MBeans, per-user statistics, transaction tracing limits and URL filtering, download access controls, in-flight statistics with optional custom/file handlers, and timer-based statistics output. It also covers ways to access stats via third-party JMX tools, the Administration Application, or the JMX download endpoint, and describes default application instrumentation. The chapter catalogs available MBeans and their metrics for server/client/core, cache, end-user browser render timing, configuration, extension points, operational/run-and-record, web services, and CER/assessment engine stats. Includes the XML schema for aggregated transaction tracing plus standard notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Cúram JMX Developer Guide
documents.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Cúram JMX Developer Guide, introducing the guide and covering legal notices. The substantive content explains how to extend Cúram JMX with custom Open MBeans to expose application operational statistics, register them in the correct runtime MBean server, and instrument code to push data only when JMX monitoring is enabled. It defines allowed Open Data types, the required MBean interface naming convention ending in StatsMBean, and optional reset() support. It also describes implementation options via CuramMBeanAbstract and built-in abstract classes for tabular stats, name-value stats, and keyed-pool stats, plus numeric counter aggregation for average/min/max/max/std dev with overflow/reset handling. The section ends with notices, privacy policy, and trademark/copyright information.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Location Administration Guide
documents.summary: Introductory front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Location Administration Guide. It identifies the guide’s purpose, intended audience (business analysts), prerequisites, and chapter map. The guide explains how organizations manage locations as a parent-child hierarchy, with only one active location structure at a time, and covers creating, activating, superseding, and canceling structures. It also previews location security and administration security using SIDs/access controls, plus general administration topics such as location resources, holiday calendars, daily schedules, translations of schedule slot names, and 14-day working patterns. The section ends with a summary and related guides, followed by legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support for Medical Assistance Long Term Care Guide
documents.summary: Overview of the Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support for Medical Assistance Long Term Care guide. It explains the business purpose and audience, then outlines Long Term Care (LTC) Medical Assistance as three coverage types: LTC, Medically Needy LTC, and Medically Needy LTC with Spend Down. The guide covers eligibility fundamentals, institutional or HCBS waiver paths, certification and financial unit rules, resource/income tests, invalid resource transfer sanctions, spousal impoverishment, post-eligibility cost of care, and expensive-home rules. It also describes eligibility determination and product delivery creation, plus change-in-circumstance reassessment for income, expense, household, and resource changes. The section includes the table of contents and ends with legal notices, privacy, and trademark/copyright information.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Multidisciplinary Team Portal Guide
documents.summary: Overview of the Cúram 8.1.2 Multidisciplinary Team Portal Guide. This front matter and first chapter introduce the MDT portal as a collaboration tool for managing cases, clients, meetings, tasks, discussions, and team contact information. It covers the home page and profile, including links to cases, clients, calendar, tasks, teams, online profile updates, and incident reporting, plus summary widgets and admin-managed login/security details. It explains collaborative case types and common functions such as discussions, meeting scheduling, minutes, notes, attachments, and multidisciplinary team membership. The guide also describes discussion participation roles, calendar use with PIM/email integration for meeting invites, meeting response statuses, recording and issuing meeting minutes, and task inbox/history handling. The remaining pages are notices and legal terms, including usage restrictions, warranty disclaimer, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Milestone Developer Guide
documents.summary: Developer guide for Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2 milestones. Explains how milestones track important case events across the case lifecycle and can be manually or automatically created/completed. Covers milestone configuration fields such as name/type, earliest start day, duration, active dates, extension/waiver flags, and optional workflow events for creation, completion, and overdue timing checks. Describes associating milestones with case/product/screening types and case components using creation/completion events, including examples like submit/approve or create/close contract. Also summarizes waiver approval setup and the main milestone entities/APIs for configuration, linking, delivery, waiver checks/requests, and business processing. The final section shows how to implement generic event handlers via handler_config.xml and Guice MapBinder/MilestoneComponentDelivery to create or complete milestone deliveries automatically at case or component level. Includes Notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Working with the Cúram Model in IBM® Rational® Software Architect Designer Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and Chapter 1 overview for the Cúram 8.1.2 guide on using IBM Rational Software Architect Designer to modify the Cúram model. Covers the Cúram profile/template, the four modeling views (Project Explorer, Properties, Diagram Editor, Model Editor), creating/opening/closing models, model fragments, UML maintenance, searching, specialized tabs/wizards, class diagrams, validation, and modeling Cúram elements such as domains, entities, structs, aggregates, process classes, and facade classes. Also includes project explorer context-menu actions by class type and broken-reference handling, including the qualified name map export/import workflow and fallback search/browse repair. The section also contains notices, privacy policy, and trademarks/legal terms.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Modeling Reference Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and the main Cúram 8.1.2 modeling reference. It introduces the UML input meta-model and generator flow, then covers packages, stereotypes, data types, audit mappings, domain definitions and overrides, entity/facade/process/struct classes, attributes, operations, mandatory parameter fields, and entity CRUD/batch/handcrafted SQL behaviors. It also includes aggregation, assign(), foreign keys, indexes/unique indexes, generated class hierarchy and factories, JMS queue connectors/field mappers, subclass modeling, and application customization guidance. Useful as the top-level entry point for the full modeling guide.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Nominees Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and main overview for the Cúram 8.1.2 Nominees Guide. It covers what a nominee is for case payments and bills, including the primary client as the default nominee, alternative nominees, and eligible participant types. It explains maintaining nominee data such as creation, delivery patterns, addresses, bank accounts, activation/deactivation, and change history. It also describes assigning case components to nominees and how financial components are generated, issued, rolled up into payments/bills, and viewed. Additional topics include reassessment for over/underpayments, reissuing canceled payments, and reviewing nominee financial transactions. The section also contains the note, edition, contents, notices, privacy policy, and trademarks pages.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Operations Guide
documents.summary: Operations guide for administering a production or production-like Cúram 8.1–8.1.2 system. Covers advanced setup: storing XSL templates for the XML Server, creating/refining a production database from generated scripts, data conversion/migration cautions, and creating organization data. Explains how to start and stop the Cúram Online System and where to find system logs. Describes batch processing via the Batch Launcher, stand-alone batch processes, core batch jobs, processing dates/parameters, and input/output interfaces. Documents configuration for the Cúram XML Server and Server Application, including dynamic vs. static properties, online administration, and publishing changes. Also covers monitoring workflow and deferred processes through Process Instances and Process Instance Errors, including suspend, stop, retry, abort, and overriding event waits. The section ends with legal notices, copyright, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Organization Administration Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and full introductory section for the Cúram 8.1.2 Organization Administration Guide. It identifies the guide’s purpose, audience, and prerequisites, then previews chapters on organization structure administration, managing/cloning structures, user administration, reporting hierarchy, and general organization administration. Core topics include the root organization unit, units, positions, user position assignment, navigation and administration security, user accounts/skills/jobs/working patterns, organizational reporting, locations, resources, web addresses, financial administration, calendars, and announcements with translation support. The conclusion summarizes key concepts and links to related Cúram and technical references. Also includes notices/legal terms, privacy policy, trademarks, copyright, and edition information for Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Participant Guide
documents.summary: Overview of the Cúram 8.1.2 Participant Guide. It introduces the participant model and 10 participant types: persons, prospect persons, employers, prospect employers, product providers, service suppliers, utilities, information providers (including educational institutes), representatives, and external parties. The guide explains participant registration, searching, and maintenance; common participant data; and type-specific details such as person/prospect person evidence, pictures, cases, incidents, deductions, eligibility, employments, employer trading status, related companies, product/service supplier data, and external party offices. It also covers merging duplicate person and prospect person records, configuration settings for search and participant behavior, and client merge customization/event points. The closing sections summarize concepts, list related guides, and provide notices/legal terms, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Person and Prospect Person Evidence Developers Guide
documents.summary: Introductory developer guide for Cúram 8.1–8.1.2 person and prospect person evidence. It explains how participant data is modeled as dynamic evidence, shared across cases, validated by Dynamic Evidence and CER, optionally brokered and verified, and replicated to legacy database tables. The guide covers prerequisites, design considerations, and a roadmap, then details dynamic evidence-to-legacy table mappings for common data such as address, bank account, birth/death, contact preferences, email, gender, identification, name, phone, relationship, and snapshot tables. It also documents customization/extension points: replicators and converters for new or existing attributes, evidence sharing automation, primary information selection, reciprocal evidence handling, and Participant Data Case owner assignment, including Guice bindings and system-user owner settings for system-created cases. The last pages are legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Personalised Pod Page Configuration Guide
documents.summary: Introductory guide for configuring Cúram 8.1.2 personalised pod pages. It explains what personal pages and pods are, how application views and user roles determine default content, and that pages must be created by developers before administrators configure them. Main topics include using the wizard to set page ID, security role, default/available pods and layout, pre-defined pods for roles, Quick Links setup and translation, user personalization actions (add/remove, hide/collapse, move, filter, refresh, reset), and performance/application properties such as lazy loading, quick links limits, and maximum pod list sizes. The section is mainly administrator-focused and includes front matter plus legal notices and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Pod Developers Guide
documents.summary: Introductory guide to developing Cúram SPM pods and pod pages. It explains the PodContainer-based UIM architecture, PodLoader responsibilities, pod/page configuration data, rendering and save flow, and prerequisites. The chapter walks through creating a host page, identifying/configuring a pod page, then building example pods: “Hello World,” a list pod, and pods with dropdown and custom text filters. It also covers adding new filter renderers, localization via text resources, and includes sample Java listings for MoviesDB and several PodLoader examples. The section is useful for developers implementing or customizing pods and related page behavior.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Provider Management Developers Guide
documents.summary: Introductory material and navigation index for the Cúram 8.1.2 Provider Management Developers Guide. It states the guide is for developers customizing CPM, distinguishes customization from configuration, and points to main extension areas: strategy-pattern service interfaces with Guice replacement modules, event hooks, workflow process definitions, products and rule sets, financial payment types, service deliveries, and compliancy/miscellaneous entities. The guide also includes Appendix A and Appendix B with XML schema definitions for import fragments (UseReference, RelatedConcept, ExternalTerm). The first pages are notice/legal content, including usage permissions, warranty disclaimer, export-law note, privacy policy, and trademarks. This node is useful as a table-of-contents-style hub for locating CPM customization APIs and related documentation.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Provider Management Configuration Guide
documents.summary:
Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Provider Management Configuration Guide. It includes the title page, edition note, and legal notices/copyright information, plus the table of contents and the main Chapter 1 overview.

The section introduces CPM provider management and its scope: provider lifecycle and stored information, service registry/service offerings, financial settings and approval criteria, provider and group maintenance, fitness checks (licenses, accreditations, home studies, background checks), facilities and placements, contracts, training/certifications, attendance rosters, incidents/investigations, provider payments and deductions, and provider self-service for inquiries, login, invoices, and rosters.

Also includes detailed notices on reproduction rights, export compliance, “AS IS” warranty/liability disclaimers, third-party product/web references, privacy policy, and trademarks. Useful as an entry point and for locating the guide’s major topics and page numbers.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Regionalization Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and Chapter 1 of the Cúram 8.1.2 Regionalization Guide. Explains how to configure regional/locale defaults for en, en_US, and en_GB, including currency, date/time formats and separators, address layout, server locale, DMX locale, and Rules Editor locale. Identifies the main files and settings locations: Application.prx, ApplicationConfiguration.properties, bootstrap.properties, environment variables, i18n resource files, project properties, and codetable files. Includes detailed settings reference tables and notes on precedence and single-default requirements for locale-related code tables (currency, nationality, country, address layout, language, locale). Ends with legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Security Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and full security guide for Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2. Covers the main security model: authentication (JAAS, default and alternate login IDs, login page, password management, identity-only/external access modes, customization), authorization (users/roles/groups, SIDs/FIDs, runtime client/server checks), cryptography (digests, ciphers, encrypted passwords, keys/keystores), and security caching. Also includes security for alternative clients (web services, batch, JMS, deferred processing), external user applications and user scope, and single sign-on setup for SAML/OpenID Connect across supported app servers. Additional sections address SSL and production hardening, HTTP verb permissions, custom authentication/authorization/cryptography, sanitization allowlists, and CSRF protection via referrer validation and the required `curam.referer.domains` setting. The tail of the document contains a CSRF behavior discussion for navigation scenarios plus full legal notices, copyright, trademarks, and sample code/license terms.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Server Developer's Guide
documents.summary: Cúram 8.1.2 Server Developer’s Guide. This node is the main entry point for server-side development and runtime reference material for the model-driven Cúram platform. It covers application/component directory structure and component precedence via SERVER_COMPONENT_ORDER, including localized component overrides. It also explains build files and targets, configuration settings, Data Manager, SQL Checker, Eclipse/SDEJ setup, logging (log4j 2), exceptions, message and code table files, deprecation, and user preferences. Runtime topics include transaction control, transaction SQL cache, deferred processing, timers, events and handlers, unique IDs, and application resource localization/fallback. Configuration chapters cover Bootstrap.properties, dynamic/static/variable properties in Application.prx, and infrastructure auditing defaults. The section is useful if you need build, configuration, localization, server API, or runtime-behavior guidance. Front and back matter include a note directing readers to Notices on page 215, legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Service Planning Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and opening of the Cúram 8.1.2 Service Planning Guide. It states the guide is for business analysts and explains the Cúram Service Planning model, with service plans used to map client needs to goals broken into manageable steps. The section previews the main chapters: overview of service planning, structured planning approach, service plan management, and service/custom service plan items. Key concepts introduced include one goal with sub-goals, plan items, milestones, integrated plans, templates, approvals, contracts, tracking, closure, and client participation. It also notes service and custom service plan items are available only with the Provider Management module and can be updated automatically from CPM delivery/attendance activity. The end of the section contains legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support for Medical Assistance Spend Down Guide
documents.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support for Medical Assistance Spend Down Guide. It states the document is a business-level overview, not a detailed user manual, for how Spend Down Medical Assistance works in Cúram. Key topics include Spend Down concepts, medically needy groups, period and budget-period rules, retroactive coverage, allowed medical expense and Pay-In evidence, eligibility checking, creation and management of Spend Down product delivery cases, reassessment after evidence changes, overpayment/potential eligibility handling, renewal limitations, and manual case closure. Also includes notices, copyright, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Summary View Developer Guide
documents.summary: Introduces the Cúram 8.1.2 Summary View Developer Guide. It explains how to build configurable summary views for eligibility decision outcomes, aimed at developers and architects familiar with the Cúram Rules Engine. The chapter covers the framework’s purpose, main components (Summary View Administrator, Builder, and Renderer), benefits, and limits. It outlines the 3-step implementation flow: configure a summary view, update a UIM page using the SummaryView/viewRulesSummary server interface, and add a link to open the view. It also describes view groups and fields (Normal, List, Linked), RDO/data item mapping, linked summary navigation, and the core entities/tables for summary view, group, and field definitions. The remaining pages are legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Supervisor Workspace Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and full contents for the Cúram 8.1.2 Supervisor Workspace Guide, plus the guide’s introduction, conclusion, and legal notices. It explains the guide’s purpose and audience, then covers how supervisors monitor and balance workloads across case, user, organization unit, and work queue workspaces. Key topics include task and user search, bulk reassignment/forwarding/reallocation, reserving and redirecting work, handling cases with issues or appeals, user settings and subscription management, schedules, and workspace customization hook points. It also documents how to exclude custom case types from supervisor views. The conclusion summarizes supported actions such as reserve, forward, reassign, block allocation, and manage user skills, working patterns, approval rules, and work queue subscriptions. The final pages contain Merative legal notices, copyright, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Configuration Transport Manager Guide
documents.summary: Overview of Cúram Configuration Transport Manager (CTM) and its documentation. Explains how CTM migrates application/configuration data as business objects across a system landscape, with a recommended minimum of separate development, test, and production systems. Covers prerequisites for setup, enabling CTM on new or existing installations, homogeneity requirements (same version, customizations, third-party software, time zone, locale, date formats, database character set), unique landscape naming, multiple landscapes, and data management policy choices, including where business objects may be created/modified and key-block range allocation. Describes the CTM change set lifecycle: define, transport/apply, clone/delete, troubleshooting, revert, and audit/history. Also includes extensive physical table-to-administration business object mappings for Platform, Infrastructure, Application Modules, Family Services, Income Support, and related assessment/outcome management areas. The last pages are legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Verification Guide
documents.summary: This section is the full Cúram 8.1.2 Verification Guide. It explains how verification works in Cúram, including the problem it solves, the three components of verification (administration, case, participant), and the main administration elements: verification categories, verifiable data items, item utilizations, requirements, requirement usages, and dependent data items. It covers caseworker workflows across the evidence lifecycle, such as capturing, deferring, viewing, verifying, modifying, and activating evidence/cases, and notes that mandatory verification must be satisfied before activation unless a waiver exists. It also documents customization points for extra case types and participants, skipping mandatory checks, waivers, and conditional verification rules, including CER rule artifacts and propagators. The section ends with legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Wait List Developer Guide
documents.summary:
Front matter and the main chapter on developing Cúram wait lists. It defines a wait list as a list of clients awaiting a resource and explains that the framework is lightweight, generic, and customizable via extension points and customizable interfaces. Key topics include the default package `curam.waitlist.impl`, replacing implementations with Guice/module bindings, renumbering logic for create/update/cancel/expire/allocate actions, and workflow events for entry created/updated/allocated/removed/expired/selected for review/deferred.

It also covers how to implement a new wait list using `WaitListType` and a `Resource` implementation, plus integration with Provider Management and Funded Program Management. Operational topics include the Expire Wait List Entry and Wait List Review batch jobs, required batch launcher settings for review-task generation, and application properties `curam.waitlist.statusreviewreminderperiod` and `curam.waitlist.statusreviewintervalperiod`. The remaining pages are notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Cúram WBPM Integration Cookbook
documents.summary: Front matter and a single integration chapter for Cúram 8.1–8.1.2. The document is a practical cookbook for integrating Cúram web services with IBM WebSphere Business Process Management tools (WID/WPS/WBPM), intended to complement the Cúram Server Modelling Guide. It covers background on WBPM components, prerequisites, and a worked example using the Cúram CreateEvidence IMT service: importing the WSDL into WID, adjusting and monitoring SOAP traffic with TCPMon, decoupling WSDL types, wrapping messages, creating and testing a WID import/process, and exposing a WID export via an Interface Map. It also includes Cúram outbound client setup, sample Java client code, and full SOAP/XML message traces showing request/response flow between Cúram inbound/outbound services and WID. The remaining pages are notices, privacy policy, and trademarks, with legal terms, warranty disclaimers, sample code permissions, and Merative copyright.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Web Client Reference Manual
documents.summary: Front matter and full contents of the Cúram 8.1.2 Web Client Reference Manual. This section includes the edition and legal notices, then indexes the entire guide: web client architecture and UIM metadata, application configuration and Smart Navigator, sections/tabs/navigation, deployment and customization, localization, session/browser management, autorecovery, domain-specific controls, custom conversion/sorting, online help, dynamic UIM maintenance and limitations, UI test automation with data-testid, and the full UIM reference. It also notes Dynamic UIM initialization behavior, including the `dynamicUIMInitModelOnStart` setting. Useful as the entry point to the manual rather than for a single technical topic.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Web Services Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and legal notices for the Cúram 8.1.2 Web Services Guide, plus Chapter 1 on integrating Cúram with external applications through Apache Axis2 web services. Covers web service basics and Cúram’s outbound connectors and inbound services, supported SOAP/WSDL styles (Document/Literal wrapped emphasized; RPC noted), and Axis2 benefits. Details building outbound stubs from WSDL, developing and packaging inbound services, and configuration files such as ws_inbound.xml and services.xml.
