Includes security guidance for WS-Security/Rampart, custom SOAP headers, HTTPS/SSL, and keystores. Also provides troubleshooting for AxisFaults, anyType issues, and general Axis2 errors. Final sections explain how to include Axis2 Admin and SOAP Monitor in the web services WAR/EAR for validation and debugging. Notices on pages 57–58 cover usage permissions, warranty disclaimers, export compliance, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Custom Widget Development Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and the full “Developing Custom Widgets” chapter for Cúram 8.1–8.1.2. It explains when to use custom widgets versus UIM, how widgets are rendered and configured, and the tradeoffs around maintenance, styling, localization, accessibility, and browser support. The chapter includes detailed examples and source code for an email address widget, photograph widget, details widget, cascading context panel widgets, a no-auto-complete text field, and a horizontal layout widget. It also covers widget anatomy, XML/path-based data access, renderer classes, renderer component model, i18n/localization, accessibility, field-level security, CSS rules, debugging/testing, and renderer configuration. The section begins with a note to read Notices and ends with legal notices, copyright/license terms, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Workflow Overview Guide
documents.summary: Introductory front matter and the main chapter on Cúram workflow. It explains workflow’s purpose: automating business processes and routing work across people/departments using process definitions, the PDT, the workflow engine, inbox/task tools, and administration. It covers when to use workflow versus API-only automation, including benefits (allocation, consistency, flexibility, traceability, customizability) and trade-offs (performance overhead, non-atomic transactions). It introduces process definition basics such as activity types, transitions, WDOs, enactment, task/notification creation, inbox queries/queues/preferences, and admin functions for monitoring instances and errors, handling events, allocation targets, work queues, and work queue name translation. The section also contains the document note, edition scope (8.1–8.1.2), contents, and legal notices/privacy/trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Workflow Reference Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and full reference guide for Cúram 8.1.2 Workflow. It applies to Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2 and maps the workflow manual’s main topics: process lifecycle and metadata, workflow data objects and enactment, activity types (automatic, event wait, manual, decision, subflow, loop, parallel), notifications, transitions, conditions, split/join, workflow structure, web service integration, file locations, runtime configuration/JMSLite, and inbox/task customization. The latter pages explain Guice-based customization of Inbox Actions, Task Actions, task search/query, and SQL generation, including the extension/binding pattern and module registration. The section also includes legal notices, permissions, warranties, export restrictions, sample code terms, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 XML Infrastructure Guide
documents.summary: This guide introduces Cúram 8.1.2 XML infrastructure for generating, transforming, and printing application data. It covers the Cúram XML DTD and runtime XML tags for structs, fields, values, and lists, plus the core APIs XMLDocument and XMLPrintStream for creating, opening, adding to, closing, saving, loading, and streaming XML. It explains XML encoding handling and the available encoding constants, with UTF-8 as default. It also describes the XML server architecture, configuration, TLS security, startup/shutdown, load balancing/failover, and statistics. The latter part focuses on XML/XSL templates for PDF, RTF, HTML, and plain text output, including job/template types, an XSL template example, generating templates from RTF, and use of XSL-FO/Xalan/FOP/JFOR. Finally, it highlights globalization issues, especially locale-sensitive serialization of Date, DateTime, and Money, and how to enable locale-neutral serialization.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Plan Items Customization Guide
documents.summary: This section introduces how to customize, develop, and implement Cúram Service Planning plan items, the building blocks of service plans. It explains the main administration settings for a plan item type, including reference, type, associated object, approval requirement, and the create/modify/view UIM pages and their parameters, plus how resolve scripts route users to those pages. It covers integrating plan items with workflow, service plans, and product deliveries, including cost handling and provider-related integration. A detailed Mileage Reimbursement example shows how custom data is stored, how approval can trigger workflow that creates, approves, and activates a product delivery, how plan-item and case records are linked, and how edit/view behavior changes once the case is active. It also notes generic compliance entities for good cause and outcome links. The section ends with legal notices, permissions, warranty disclaimers, privacy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Data Mapping Editor Configuration Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and opening chapter of the Cúram 8.1.2 Data Mapping Editor Configuration Guide. It states the guide’s purpose, audience, and prerequisites, then explains data mapping for moving CDS data into evidence entities for program mappings on intake applications. The chapter covers creating, editing, releasing, deleting, and maintaining multiple effective-dated mapping configurations, noting that released mappings are immutable and newer mappings with the same effective date supersede older ones. It also describes the Data Mapping Editor UI: title bar, menu bar, information panel, datastore entity tree, evidence entity list, and mapping workspace. The guide explains how to create entity and attribute mappings with drag-and-drop, add conditions and set values, use code table mappings, and delete mappings; it also notes invalid attribute mapping rules and that mappings cannot be edited after creation. The section includes notices, privacy policy, trademarks, and legal/copyright information.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Designing Evidence Guide
documents.summary:
Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Designing Evidence Guide. It includes the introductory note, edition statement, table of contents, and legal notices/trademarks/privacy information.

The substantive content introduces evidence design in Cúram and explains the key choice between dynamic evidence (recommended; configurable in the admin app; supports effective dating and shared storage) and static evidence (mainly for special cases such as encryption; separate tables; more manual implementation and validations). It then covers how to model real-world data as evidence: defining attributes and hierarchies, reusing delivered types, handling multiple instances, effective-date changes, evidence groups, verification, sharing between person and case levels, and multi-participant evidence.

The section also previews sample designs for Address, Employment, SSN, and Medical Condition evidence, plus delivered person-level evidence types such as birth/death, gender, name, identifications, bank accounts, postal addresses, email, phone, contact preferences, and relationships.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Decision Assist Developer Guide
documents.summary: Opening/front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Decision Assist Developer Guide. It identifies the guide, edition scope (Cúram 8.1–8.1.2), prerequisites, and sample business scenarios used throughout (family risk, youth corrections, abuse, family services, partner maltreatment, asthma). It then outlines the main development/admin areas: DeterminationConfig, DeterminationPackage, Questionnaire, DecisionMatrix, Outcomes, TypicalPicture, Condition, ICDCode, and related widgets; assessment-side classes/widgets; integration with assessments, integrated cases, IEG, and case/participant data; batch processing including ICD code upload and processingDate behavior; and question-text localization via QUESTION, LOCALIZABLETEXT, and TEXTTRANSLATION with fallback to default text. The section also includes the table of contents plus legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Using the Data Mapping Engine Guide
documents.summary: Introductory guide to the Cúram 8.1.2 Data Mapping Engine. Explains how intake/screening data is stored in the datastore and mapped to evidence entities, PDF application forms, and third-party participants. Covers the data mapping environment and development workflow, logical maps, and how to write mapping specifications/configurations for static evidence, PDFs, and dynamic evidence. Also includes PDF form configuration, XML schema references for mapping specs/configs, and troubleshooting guidance with error logging. The final sections list APROCER001–042 error codes and show how to enable more detailed logging, plus advice on handling validation with minimal/defaulted intake values. Front matter includes notices, edition, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Developing Static Evidence Guide
documents.summary: Cúram 8.1.2 Developing Static Evidence Guide covers how to build static evidence either manually or with the Evidence Generator. It explains the evidence framework, required server/client artifacts, interface hierarchy, controller hooks, and core lifecycle operations such as create, modify, view, transfer, attribution, approval, succession, and participant handling. The guide details generator inputs/outputs, evidence patterns, required modeling, customization points, XML/properties metadata, build/configuration workflow, and troubleshooting. It also includes reference API examples for StandardEvidenceInterface, EvidenceInterface, and ParticipantEvidenceInterface, plus common compilation, metatype, and property-file generation errors. Front matter includes a note, edition applicability (Cúram 8.1–8.1.2), contents, and legal notices/privacy/trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Persistence Cookbook
documents.summary: Cúram 8.1.2 Persistence Cookbook is a practical guide to using and extending the Persistence Infrastructure for service-layer APIs. It explains the façade-to-service data translation model and is aimed at developers of server functionality and callers of PI-based APIs. The main content covers how to read, insert, modify, delete/cancel, and list rows; how to define entity and DAO interfaces and implementations; validation rules and defaults; Guice module setup; events and listeners; entity context; state transitions; inheritance mappings; and adding new read-only searches to shipped entities. It includes scenarios plus implementation patterns, with JavaDoc as the detailed reference. The book also contains legal notices, privacy policy, trademarks, and sample-code permissions.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Implementing the Eligibility Viewer Guide
documents.summary: Guide for implementing the Cúram 8.1.2 Eligibility Viewer (EV) for integrated cases. Covers the EV purpose and navigation tabs, the required SPI (`TimelineCalendarDataRetrieval`) and returned data model (`TimelineCalendarRow`, `TimelineCalendarTimeline`, `TimelineCalendarIntervalValue`), and binding the SPI to an integrated case type with Guice/`MODULECLASSNAME.dmx`. Explains how to add the EV navigation page (`EligibilityGraph`), enable it with `curam.core.timelinecalendar.enabled=true`, and configure display settings in `TimelineCalendar.properties` (row titles and interval colors). Also describes key events: customizing evidence-based event messages and adding non-evidence-based key events using separate retriever interfaces. Front matter, notices, privacy policy, and trademarks are included but are mostly legal/reference material.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Evidence Broker Guide
documents.summary: Introductory chapter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Evidence Broker Guide. Explains how the evidence broker shares evidence between cases, why sharing is needed, and core terminology. Covers evidence sharing between cases, shared data sets, matching and maintenance patterns, sharing rules, identical vs logically equivalent evidence, verification sharing, triggers, incoming list review, and end-to-end scenarios. Also describes configuration of sharing and matching, custom matching and delivery-plan filters, controls for when to share or not share evidence, paid employment special handling, pull-workflow setup, troubleshooting via shared instance search and trace logging, and generated workflow tasks for failed activation or manual review. Includes front matter, contents, notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Getting Started Evidence Guide
documents.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Getting Started Evidence Guide. It includes a note to read the Notices section first, edition applicability for Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2, and the table of contents. The substantive content begins with Chapter 1, “Evidence,” which defines evidence as information used to determine client or household eligibility and outlines a getting-started guide for developers and administrators. Topics referenced include designing evidence, dynamic vs. static evidence, extracting dynamic evidence artefacts, person evidence, correction vs. succession, sharing and verifying evidence, maintenance, lifecycle, security administration, and creating dynamic evidence types. It also provides a brief introduction to the key business flow for evidence. The remaining pages are legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support Food Assistance Program Guide
documents.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support Food Assistance Program Guide. It introduces the Food Assistance eligibility rules and is aimed at business analysts, not technical administration. The guide covers the full determination flow: non-financial requirements (citizenship/alienage, SSN, residency), assistance unit and household composition rules, alien sponsor deeming and battered-alien exceptions, resource and income calculations, benefit computation, and sanction assessment. It also includes sanctions for controlled substance use, intentional program violations, work non-compliance, voluntary quit, child support enforcement, drug-related felony, fleeing felon, parole violation, fraud, firearms sales, and trafficking. Pages 45–46 contain legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks only.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Health Care Reform Business Guide
documents.summary: Cúram 8.1.2 Health Care Reform Business Guide for Income Support for Medical Assistance. This node is the full manual, covering ACA/Exchange support, MAGI-based eligibility, citizen portal and caseworker intake, human services screening and applications, plan shopping/enrollment, appeals, change of circumstance, renewals and PDM, and deprecated monitoring reports. It also includes HCR program information with configurable rate tables for federal poverty levels and tax-filing thresholds, plus legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks. Useful as the top-level navigation entry for core business flows and reference/configuration topics.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Health Care Reform Developer Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and legal notices for the Cúram 8.1.2 Health Care Reform Developer Guide, followed by the full contents and the main guide. The section is a navigation/implementation overview for customizing HCR features: adding products and Streamlined Medicaid categories; configuring Eligibility Viewer, Universal Access, appeals, resilient intake, and primary client display; customizing the citizen portal; integrating with external systems/Federal Hub/state systems and electronic verification; case management, plan management, change of circumstances, evidence wizards, appeal requests, closed-case reassessment, trigger points, periodic data matching/annual renewals, inconsistency period processing, FFE account transfer, monitoring, bulk reassessment, and intake address mapping. The end pages include Merative usage terms, export restrictions, warranty disclaimers, patent/trademark notices, and copyright © 2012, 2024.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 How to Build a Product Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and opening section for the Cúram 8.1.2 “How to Build a Product” guide. It introduces the guide’s purpose for designers/developers and explains building products through examples around eligibility, entitlement, and explanation. Key topics include defining product requirements, evidence capture, rules/calculations, results display, security, testing, and performance. It also covers a simple product walkthrough, configuring the product in the Administration Workspace, decision details rules and dynamic UIM display pages, and a deprecated key decision factors feature. The section includes related documentation references, a detailed table of contents, and legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 IEG in the Cúram Universal Access Responsive Web Application
documents.summary: Guide for Cúram 8.1.2 Universal Access Responsive Web Application IEG. It explains that evidence-gathering forms now run in the browser via the IEG React Player, so existing scripts may need retesting and minor changes. The section covers design-system-specific IEG elements/attributes, unsupported authoring features, and customization patterns for the Back button, section navigation, progress text, dynamic titles, rich text/external links, hint and explainer text, help label text, required/optional labels, input masks and constraints, code-table hierarchies, combo boxes, BaseFormContainer scripting, cluster merging, relationship-page labels and dates, and quick-add-list. It also includes legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support Business Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and legal pages for the Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support Business Guide, covering the product scope, overview, contents, and notices through page 108. The guide explains Income Support for food, cash, and medical assistance, including intake, eligibility determination, benefit calculation, online citizen applications, shared evidence, and key business flows. It also highlights program rules for SNAP-like food assistance, TANF-style cash assistance, and Medicaid/CHIP support, plus configuration topics such as eligibility start dates, certification periods, expedited verification, Eligibility Viewer, primary client settings, concurrent eligibility detection, evidence wizard customization, and MAGI/non-MAGI cascading eligibility hooks. The remaining pages are legal notices, permissions, warranty disclaimers, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Inside Eligibility and Entitlement Using Cúram Express Rules Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and reference content for the Cúram 8.1.2 “Inside Eligibility and Entitlement Using Cúram Express Rules” guide. It introduces the guide’s purpose and audience, then acts as a roadmap to the main chapter covering eligibility/entitlement processing, determination navigation, calculation and display of eligibility/decision details, rule object converters/propagators, storage, financial scheduling, reassessment, incremental design, compliancy, engine API/customizability, CER extensions, caching, and environment variables. It also includes the guide’s edition scope (Cúram 8.1–8.1.2) and legal notices, copyright, privacy policy, and trademarks. Useful mainly as a navigation and legal reference node, not substantive implementation content.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Intake Guide
documents.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Intake Guide. It identifies the edition as applying to Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2, and points readers to the Notices section before use. The main content introduces Cúram Intake as a flexible “No Wrong Door” framework for triage, screening, applications, and referrals across multiple programs. It covers client identification and registration, triage outcomes, screening for potential eligibility, the lifecycle of an application case (creation through closure, statuses, eligibility, authorization, PCR assessment, timers, and contact management), intake issues (verification, evidence validation, authorization failures), referrals to external agencies or service providers, and worker tools such as Advisor, Notes, Application Search, My Applications, Referral Search, My Referrals, and My Programs Due. The final pages contain Merative notices, warranty/export disclaimers, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Intake Configuration Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and the main intake configuration chapter for Cúram 8.1.2. It includes a notice to read the legal notices first, edition info for Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2, and the full table of contents. The substantive content configures Cúram Intake triage/screening: application case types, home pages, eligibility check rulesets/strategies, ownership and evidence-issue ownership, resilience flow, display options, Ready for Determination, direct creation, related cases, add-client behavior, PCR, program association/evidence types, internal application forms, submission/client registration rules, and program evidence mappings/reuse of product delivery cases. It also covers person search and match configuration: criteria, weights, thresholds, maximum hits, and related system properties, plus legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Intake Developer's Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and Chapter 1 of the Cúram 8.1.2 Intake Developer’s Guide. It introduces Intake development and explains the required intake process XML plus supporting configurations for triage, screenings, eligibility checks, PCR rule sets, and authorization rule sets. The chapter covers how to define triage and screening in XML, including rule-set attributes, urgent alerts, and recommendations for services/programs. It also describes check-eligibility configuration, PCR rule attributes and localized property text, and authorization rule sets for selecting programs and clients during application-case authorization. Additional topics include the intake process XML structure, a hook for activating evidence not normally brokered during authorization, and intake environment properties such as Google Maps defaults. The section also includes notices, copyright, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Cúram Income Support for Medical Assistance Program Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and overview for the Cúram 8.1–8.1.2 Income Support for Medical Assistance guide. Explains the rule-based eligibility framework used to determine medical assistance coverage, including cascading eligibility, non-financial requirements, household composition, income/resource rules, deeming, deductions, and coverage-specific financial limits and periods. Covers major program types such as LIFC, transitional and extended LIFC, children, pregnant women, newborn, Title IV-E foster/adoption, medically needy, refugee, ABD, Medicare cost-sharing programs (QMB/SLMB/QI/QDWI), breast/cervical cancer, emergency medical assistance, and retroactive requests. Also includes edition information, table of contents, and legal front matter: notices, privacy policy, trademarks, and copyright.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Cúram REST API Guide
documents.summary: Introduction and full guide for Cúram 8.1.2 REST API integration. It covers inbound REST APIs for mobile and external systems, using existing Social Program REST APIs via Swagger, authentication, GET/POST/PUT/DELETE usage, JSON responses, path/query parameters, security, data types, troubleshooting, and domain APIs. It then explains how to develop custom inbound REST APIs, including API design, creating Social Program REST APIs, reference material, and GraphQL. The outbound section shows how to call external REST services with Jersey, including JSON serialization, client setup, authentication, deployment/build steps, request filters, and HTTPS/SSL handling, plus application-server classpath/JAR conflict notes. Also includes notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Outcome Management Configuration Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and chapter 1 of the Cúram 8.1.2 Outcome Management Configuration Guide. It explains how administrators configure Outcome Management assessments and outcome plans: assessment definitions, intervals, reassessment, approval checks, categories/classifications, roles, factors, case associations, security, and result display options (graphs, guidance, scores, priorities). It also covers outcome plan setup, including goals, objectives, types, activities, progress, recommendation conditions, system properties, and security. Later sections address dynamic products, CDA and CER assessment examples, and technical notes on CER rules execution, IEG datastore/schema requirements, and pre/post-populate hooks. The document is aimed at system administrators familiar with code tables, application properties, and security. The node also includes notices, copyright, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Outcome Management Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and main content for the Cúram 8.1.2 Outcome Management Guide. Introduces outcome management as a client-centric, collaborative approach focused on desired client results, then explains how to configure and use assessments and outcome plans to identify needs, build goals/objectives/activities, assign MDT teams, manage agreements and visitation plans, and monitor progress through reassessments, reviews, reporting, notes, attachments, correspondence, related cases, and productivity views. Also covers completing actions/services/goals/objectives and closing outcome plans. The section includes the note, edition, contents, and legal notices/privacy/trademark pages. Useful if you need either the conceptual model or operational workflow for outcome management, plus the publication’s usage/legal terms.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Priority Complexity Risk Configuration Guide
documents.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Priority Complexity Risk Configuration Guide. It includes the title, note, edition/purpose, table of contents, and full legal pages (notices, privacy policy, trademarks). The guide targets administrators configuring PCR processing and covers: creating a PCR configuration, associating it with a case type, deferred/manual/automatic assessments, configuration lifecycle and publish rules, PCR range mappings, and building PCR rule sets in CER. It also lists required PCR rule attributes (caseID, description, priority/complexity/risk rates and reasons) and explains how to supply localized rule-set text via a properties file and Dynamic UIM resource store entry.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Person Evidence Management
documents.summary: This section documents Cúram 8.1.2 Person Evidence Management. It explains the person-centric evidence lifecycle for persons and prospect persons, including registration, in-edit vs active evidence, mandatory evidence blocking completion, verifications, evidence sharing, case-management validations, program eligibility use, participant search/merge, and person owner assignment. It also covers configuration in administration: enabling Person Evidence Management, setting up evidence sharing, verification rules (due/expiry dates, waivers, conditional rules), and smart navigator. The development section describes the PEC layer on top of PDC, the `PECCommonUtil.isInitialPersonRegistrationComplete` API, and how to extend PEC-to-PDC replication for custom evidence via replicator extenders, including implementation, Guice binding, module registration, and redeployment. The remaining pages are legal notices, copyright, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 REST API Accelerator Guide
documents.summary: This section is the full Cúram 8.1.2 REST API Accelerator Guide. It introduces the REST API accelerator built on Cúram Platform 6.1, Outcome Management, and the Adult Social Care accelerator, and explains how to integrate an external mobile app via Cúram REST services. It covers the main personas (business analyst, Cúram developer, mobile app developer), architecture using IBM MobileFirst, accelerator overview, installation, requirements analysis, and creating custom REST APIs. It also includes appendix material with concrete API examples for retrieving case details and handling dynamic evidence, including practitioner certificates and nested diagnosis records, with create/update/delete flows and required child-first deletion ordering. The final pages contain Merative notices, licensing/permissions, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support for Return to Work Outcome Management Guide
documents.summary:
Front matter and main content for the Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support for Return to Work Outcome Management Guide. It targets business analysts in social enterprise organizations and explains the client-centric outcome management approach for clients referred to employment/outcomes agencies. The guide covers purpose, audience, prerequisites, and related guides, then walks through outcome services referrals, self-sufficiency assessments, advisements, outcome plan creation/approval/closure, client agreements, attendance and participation tracking, discrepancy handling, case reviews, and productivity tools.

It also describes worker roles (eligibility worker, outcome worker, supervisor), integration with the Income Support case, support for single or multi-person plans, and configurable summaries for overdue assessments and discrepancies. The section includes the contents page and end matter: notices, privacy policy, trademarks, and legal/copyright terms.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Reviewing and Processing Evidence Guide
documents.summary: Introductory front matter and chapter 1 of the Cúram 8.1.2 Reviewing and Processing Evidence Guide. It explains how caseworkers review and maintain evidence using the main evidence lists (person tab, application, evidence type, active, in-edit, incoming, verifications, issues), dashboard and evidence object views, and actions such as create, modify, delete/discard, validate, and apply changes. It also covers evidence sharing/compare-and-resolve, parent/child evidence display, and when verifications or issues are available. The section includes detailed guidance for Multiple Participant Evidence Update for dynamic evidence, including supported page behavior, constraints (not compatible with parent-child evidence or Show All Fields), and performance considerations. The remaining pages are notices, privacy policy, and trademarks, with legal terms, warranty disclaimers, export compliance, and copyright information.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Social Enterprise Collaboration Configuration Guide
documents.summary: Configuration guide for Cúram 8.1–8.1.2 Social Enterprise Collaboration. Covers how to configure social enterprise folders and multidisciplinary teams: SEF types, predefined vs folder-defined MDTs, team member roles, sharing and sensitivity controls, supported case types, SEF relationships, MDT notifications, and portal display limits. Also explains discussion settings such as default moderator, pagination, comments per page, email links, and watcher notifications. Includes compliance guidance and transaction-history logging for SEF/MDT events, with application properties to disable specific transactions. Ends with summary tables of SEF/MDT and discussion configuration properties plus legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Social Enterprise Collaboration Configuration Guide
documents.summary: Introduces the Cúram 8.1.2 Social Enterprise Collaboration Configuration Guide. The section explains Merative SPM Social Enterprise Collaboration as a common platform for multidisciplinary agencies, providers, and community partners to improve client and family outcomes. It covers the main tools and concepts: MDT Portal, Social Enterprise Folder (SEF), Citizen Context Viewer (CCV), collaborative outcome plans, collaborative investigation cases, and the deprecated Cúram Case and Participant Index (CPI). It also describes why multidisciplinary teams are needed, how predefined vs. case-specific teams collaborate, and how sharing, meetings, discussions, notes, attachments, and access controls work. CPI is presented as a deprecated cross-system data repository for person and case indexing, search, retrieval, and modernization support for legacy systems. Front matter includes edition information for Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2, contents, notices, privacy policy, trademarks, and legal usage terms.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Cúram on Kubernetes
documents.summary: Introductory front matter and deployment guide for Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2. It explains containerized Cúram on IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service using WebSphere Application Server Liberty, Docker, Helm, and IBM MQ LTS, including the roles of each technology and support/version notes. It then covers Kubernetes architecture changes needed for cloud-native deployment: transaction isolation, JMS producer/consumer separation, EJB local interfaces, MQ constraints, and elasticity. The Liberty deployment section covers prerequisite software, deployment properties, server configuration, web server plug-in, security and SSO, building and installing EAR/web service artifacts, JSP precompilation, deployment verification, remote debugging, JMX, and known limitations such as no collectives and unsupported dropins. Also includes notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Cúram Platform Analytics Accelerator Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and main guide for the Cúram 8.1.2 Platform Analytics Accelerator. It introduces the SPMP Analytics accelerator as a worked BIRT example built on Adult Social Care, centered on a caseworker scenario with default charts for assigned Sickness Benefit vs. Incapacity Benefit cases and clients with multiple diagnoses. The section explains the platform/business process, when to choose SPMP Analytics versus SPMP BI&A based on data volume, workload, and scalability needs, and covers the full accelerator lifecycle: installation prerequisites, BIRT/Eclipse setup, scenario testing, fit-gap and requirements analysis, chart development, pod/workspace customization, publishing/testing, and preserving customizations via DMX/source control. Also includes the front-matter Note, Edition, Contents, and legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 System Administration Guide
documents.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 System Administration Guide, covering pages 1–48. It includes the title page, a note to read Notices first, edition info for Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2, the table of contents, Chapter 1 “Configuring the system,” and the Notices/Privacy policy/Trademarks pages. The chapter introduces system administration and covers configuring the Rich Text Editor to filter corrupted note content, application properties, code tables, configurable validations, text translations and locale mappings, autorecovery, contact logs, notes, participant nicknames, localizable display rules, case audit selection queries, communication templates, batch processes, security, Business Intelligence reports/viewer, target systems, content management interoperability, Microsoft Word templates, and FILE_EDIT integration for Chrome/Edge. It also includes Word Integration Assistant installation, environment variables/registry settings, and troubleshooting scripts. Notices cover reproduction/use terms, export and warranty disclaimers, patent notice, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Troubleshooting Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and main troubleshooting content for Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2. Covers systematic problem diagnosis, including describing symptoms, tracing logs across components, isolating the failing layer, timing/conditions, reproducibility, and collecting support data such as versions, configs, logs, XML server logs, and database connectivity details. Includes guidance for blank/frozen pages, batch process failures, contacting Cúram Support, and submitting enhancement requests via the Merative Ideas Portal. A notable workaround addresses the “double load” modal resolver-page issue, including `DISMISS_MODAL="false"`. The known limitations section documents several UI and rules/CER issues: empty tab containers, Dynamic UIM modal wizard page-id conflicts, file download failures, default server error-page behavior, hidden action sets in modal SVG dialogs, `\n` property and rules widget errors, validation message ordering, restricted propagator classes, CER comment stripping, and CER export display/progress quirks. Includes notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Understanding Evidence Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and chapter 1 of the Cúram 8.1.2 Understanding Evidence Guide. Includes the Note, Edition, Contents, Notices, Privacy policy, and Trademarks sections plus the full introductory chapter, “Understanding evidence.” The chapter defines evidence as client-supplied information used with rules to determine eligibility/entitlement, explains why evidence is collected, how it evolves over time, how it is shared or restricted across cases/programs, and when it must be verified. It covers evidence capture via online, paper, and IEG channels; maintenance in prospect person, person, and case records; dynamic vs static evidence screens; predefined person evidence; extending and relating evidence types; recording changes and correction/succession history; approval workflows for sensitive evidence; and one-time evidence transfer versus ongoing sharing between cases.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Working with Cúram Express Rules Guide
documents.summary: Front matter and Chapter 1 of the Cúram 8.1.2 Working with Cúram Express Rules guide. It introduces CER Rules Editor, its audience (business analysts and technical developers), and editor layout. The chapter covers creating a simple rule set, classes, attributes, folders, rules, calculations, organizing, exporting diagrams, validation, publishing, and execution. It also explains point-in-time business rules, how technical developers extend them with implementation details, timeline-aware rules and existence timelines, and testing by extracting rule sets/resources, generating test classes, and writing/running JUnit tests. Includes notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

documents.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Working with Intelligent Evidence Gathering (IEG) Guide
documents.summary: Introductory guide to Cúram 8.1.2 Intelligent Evidence Gathering (IEG). It explains IEG’s purpose for capturing client-related evidence in applications, the core architecture (Engine/Player, Datastore, Resource Store), and how to design, evaluate, build, validate, and run scripts. Main topics include household and relationship capture, composite and associated data, summary pages, efficient data-entry patterns (lists, code tables, conditional elements, matrices, fast path, implicit delete, date picker), read-only behavior, custom functions, reusable subscripts, versioning, and custom HTML on summaries. It also covers integrating IEG into Cúram apps (tab/modal, redirection, cleanup, resuming), managing captured datastore data, using the Resource Store, scanning with IBM Rational AppScan, and runtime behavior during network loss/offline use. Front matter includes notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.
